channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The US must return a Maryland man mistakenly deported to an El Salvador prison, judge says
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge on Friday ordered the Trump administration to arrange for the return of a Maryland man to the United States after he was mistakenly deported to a notorious El Salvador prison, while a U.S. government attorney was at a loss to explain what happened. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump to give TikTok another 75-day extension
item.description: President Trump said Friday that he plans to give TikTok another 75-day extension as his administration nears a deal to avert a ban on the popular social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Texas measles outbreak approaches 500 cases
item.description: The deadly measles outbreak in Texas is continuing to rise, now approaching a total of 500 confirmed cases with at least 56 people facing hospitalization since late January, according to health officials.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: 100-year-old tortoise gives birth to critically endangered hatchlings at Philly Zoo
item.description: For the first time in over 150 years, the Philadelphia Zoo has new additions to the Western Santa Cruz Galapagos tortoise family.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, Fox 5

item.title: US must return Maryland father mistakenly deported to El Salvador prison, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge on Friday ordered the Trump administration to arrange for the return of a Maryland man to the United States after he was mistakenly deported to a notorious El Salvador prison.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly half of National Weather Service offices have 20% vacancy rates, and experts say it's a risk
item.description: Data obtained by The Associated Press shows that close to half of the local National Weather Service forecast offices have 20% vacancy rates as severe weather chugs across the nation’s heartland.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tornadoes will become more frequent in coming weeks. Here's what to know if your home is destroyed
item.description: An average of 1,200 tornadoes hit the United States yearly, and the country will probably get more killer supercells spawning tornadoes and hail as the world warms, according to a 2023 study.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Viral video shows Florida charter captain arguing with boater; police investigating
item.description: A viral video showing a fight between two Florida boaters has sparked both a police investigation and strong reactions from people online.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bondi announces charges in MS-13 killing as the Trump administration highlights its gang crackdown
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Three alleged MS-13 gang members have been federally charged in connection with a killing a decade ago in Florida, U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi announced Friday, seeking to highlight the Trump administration's push to prosecute violent gangs.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas Pete hot sauces recalled in 10 states
item.description: The T.W. Garner Food Company issued a recall for certain bottles of its Texas Pete Habanero Buffalo Sauce and Texas Pete Sweet CHAbanero Sauce on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Reiner, WGN 9

item.title: Trump ordered to pay legal fees in ‘Steele Dossier’ lawsuit
item.description: A judge in the United Kingdom has ordered President Donald Trump to pay more than $820,000 in legal fees to the company representing former British spy Christopher Steele after he unsuccessfully sued over a dossier.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: China to impose a 34% tariff on imports of all US products
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China announced Friday that it will impose a 34% tariff on imports of all U.S. products beginning April 10, part of a flurry of retaliatory measures following U.S. President Donald Trump’s “Liberation Day” slate of double-digit tariffs. The new tariff matches the rate of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Federal officials are quietly terminating the legal residency of some international college students
item.description: A crackdown on foreign students is alarming college leaders, who say the Trump administration is using new tactics and vague justifications to push some students out of the country.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Annie Ma And Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dad claims son was harassed by staff for wearing 'Let's Go Brandon' shirt, sues school district
item.description: A father has filed a lawsuit against an Ohio school district, claiming his son was punished for wearing a "Let's Go Brandon" T-shirt to school.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump sued over China tariffs
item.description: It marks the first known legal challenge against Trump’s tariffs, which have fulfilled a campaign promise and rattled financial markets.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KRON4

item.title: University of South Carolina student, 21, killed in hit-and-run crash involving illegal immigrant: DHS
item.description: Nathanial "Nate" G. Baker was killed after being struck on his motorcycle by a truck driven by Rosali Fernandez-Cruz, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: In the latest poll, 37 percent of respondents approved of Trump’s handling of the economy and 30 percent approved of his approach to addressing the high cost of living in the country.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Pentagon watchdog to review Hegseth’s use of Signal app to convey plans for Houthi strike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon’s acting inspector general announced Thursday that he would review Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s use of the Signal messaging app to convey plans for a military strike against Houthi militants in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: K-12 schools must sign certification against DEI to receive federal money, administration says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As a condition for receiving federal money, the Trump administration is ordering K-12 schools to certify that they are following federal civil rights laws and ending any discriminatory diversity, equity and inclusion practices.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Transgender student's arrest for violating Florida bathroom law is thought to be a first
item.description: A transgender college student declared “I am here to break the law” before entering a women’s restroom at the Florida State Capitol and being led out in handcuffs by police.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired from CDC
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those fired from the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Senate confirms Oz as head of CMS along party lines
item.description: The Senate has confirmed Mehmet Oz as administrator for the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS), making him the head of Medicaid at a time where its funding is at risk of being severely cut. The Senate voted 53-45 along party lines to confirm Oz.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Tornadoes, winds, flooding and hail: Arkansas wakes up to disastrous severe storm damage
item.description: Communities across Arkansas are waking up to severe storm damage as the state continues to prepare for more potential severe weather later this week.
item.dc:creator: Cate Skinner, NewsNation

item.title: Staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired from CDC
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those who were fired from the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), a now former employee told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, ABC 4

item.title: Why you won't be able to send or receive money using the Zelle app
item.description: If you used Zelle's app to send or receive money, you'll now have to use your financial institution’s mobile app or go through online banking.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, NewsNation

item.title: Tony's Chocolonely issues recall after 'small stones' found in chocolate bars
item.description: Tony's Chocolonely Inc. issued a voluntary recall for its Tony's brand Dark Almond Sea Salt Bar and Everything Bar because the products may contain small stones. The two bars were sold nationwide via retail stores and www.tonyschocolonely.com. The product was distributed from Feb. 7 to March 24.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Yankees OF Brett Gardner's son died of carbon monoxide poisoning, had 'layer' on organs, officials say
item.description: Carbon monoxide poisoning was the cause of death of the teenage son of former New York Yankees outfielder Brett Gardner, authorities in Costa Rica confirmed Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Markets plunge in reaction to Trump 'Liberation Day' tariffs
item.description: Stocks plunged Thursday morning as U.S. trading opened for the first time after President Trump’s announcement of heavy tariffs on nearly every nation exporting products to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Dow drops 1,600 as US stocks lead worldwide sell-off after Trump’s tariffs cause a COVID-like shock
item.description: Financial markets around the world are reeling Thursday following President Donald Trump’s latest and most severe set of tariffs, and the U.S. stock market is taking the worst of it so far.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Dow drops 1,600 as US stocks lead worldwide sell-off after Trump’s tariffs cause a COVID-like shock
item.description: Financial markets around the world are reeling Thursday following President Donald Trump’s latest and most severe set of tariffs, and the U.S. stock market is taking the worst of it so far.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Juice sold from New York to wineries in 12 states recalled over potentially deadly toxin
item.description: A wine juice wholesaler in New York has recalled a pumpkin wine juice sold to wineries in a dozen states because it contains a toxin that could be deadly.
item.dc:creator: Madalyn Buursma, Fox 5

item.title: Cheer on the Cougars: UH Final Four game watch party locations
item.description: The University of Houston Cougars have are making their way to the NCAA Men’s Final Four in San Antonio.
item.dc:creator: Kendall Mayes, KPRC 2

item.title: Tariffs expected to hike up costs of Toyota trucks made in Baja
item.description: "I expect prices to go up probably in a few weeks," he said. "It will also be more expensive for people to service and repair their cars since many parts come from Mexico and China.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KTLA 5

item.title: Arrests made in drunken assault, theft of beloved Paddington Bear statue
item.description: Two 22-year-old men who were reportedly intoxicated when they vandalized and stole a statue of Paddington Bear eating marmalade sandwich on a park bench in Newbury, England have been issued steep fines and sentenced to community service, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: Charges dismissed against Illinois man accused of assault by Rep. Nancy Mace
item.description: Federal prosecutors moved to dismiss charges against a man U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of assaulting her during an event at the Capitol last year.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Brams, Fox 5

item.title: Scientists fear impact of National Weather Service cuts
item.description: As the summer hurricane season looms, so too do cuts to the federal workforce, including the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, Fox 5
